name: 6-WEEK, RANDOMIZED, DOUBLE-BLIND, SPONSOR-OPEN STUDY TO ASSESS THE EFFECT OF REPEATED SUBCUTANEOUS ADMINISTRATION OF PF-06946860 ON APPETITE IN PARTICIPANTS WITH ADVANCED CANCER AND ANOREXIA, FOLLOWED BY AN 18-WEEK OPEN-LABEL TREATMENT PERIOD

titles.text: 6-WEEK, RANDOMIZED, DOUBLE-BLIND, SPONSOR-OPEN STUDY TO ASSESS THE EFFECT OF REPEATED SUBCUTANEOUS ADMINISTRATION OF PF-06946860 ON APPETITE IN PARTICIPANTS WITH ADVANCED CANCER AND ANOREXIA, FOLLOWED BY AN 18-WEEK OPEN-LABEL TREATMENT PERIOD
Study to Assess the Effect of PF-06946860 on Appetite Following Subcutaneous Administration to Patients With Anorexia and Advanced Cancer

versions.rationale: This study aims to assess the effect of repeated subcutaneous administration of PF-06946860 on appetite in participants with advanced cancer and anorexia.

population.name: Main Study Population
population.label: patients with advanced cancer, anorexia and elevated GDF-15 levels
population.description: This study, therefore, intends to enroll patients with advanced cancer, anorexia and elevated GDF-15 levels. Across malignancies, cachexia is highly prevalent, impacting approximately half of patients with advanced cancer, including those solid tumor types selected for inclusion in this study. Elevated levels of circulating GDF-15 have been reported in literature in patients with a variety of tumor types experiencing anorexia and weight loss.

arms.name: PF-06946860
arms.label: PF-06946860
arms.description: Participants who meet the entry criteria will be randomized to study drug (PF-06946860). The 6-week double-blind period will consist of a total of 2 SC doses, administered 3 weeks apart (Q3W). The 6-week double-blind treatment period will be followed by optional open-label treatment (OLT) with PF-06946860 of up to 18 weeks; the total duration of treatment would, therefore, be up to 24 weeks.

arms.name: Placebo
arms.label: Placebo
arms.description: Participants who meet the entry criteria will be randomized to study drug (placebo). The 6-week double-blind period will consist of a total of 2 SC doses, administered 3 weeks apart (Q3W). The 6-week double-blind treatment period will be followed by optional open-label treatment (OLT) with PF-06946860 of up to 18 weeks; the total duration of treatment would, therefore, be up to 24 weeks.

studyDesigns.name: Phase 1b Parallel Group with Optional Open-Label Extension
studyDesigns.label: Phase 1b study in patients with advanced cancer, anorexia and elevated circulating GDF-15 levels

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening
epochs.description: Potential participants will attend 2 clinic visits (Screening and Randomization) with the investigator. The Screening visit will occur no more than 28 days and no less than 5 days, prior to Randomization. The 5-day minimum duration between Screening and Randomization visits will ensure that all necessary data (including GDF-15 level) are available to inform the investigator's assessment of eligibility.

epochs.name: Double-Blind Treatment
epochs.label: 6-week double-blind treatment period
epochs.description: The initial 6-week treatment period will be a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel group study. Participants who meet the entry criteria will be randomized to study drug (PF-06946860 or placebo). The 6-week double-blind period will consist of a total of 2 SC doses, administered 3 weeks apart (Q3W).

epochs.name: Open-Label Treatment
epochs.label: optional open-label treatment (OLT)
epochs.description: The 6-week double-blind treatment period will be followed by optional open-label treatment (OLT) with PF-06946860 of up to 18 weeks; the total duration of treatment would, therefore, be up to 24 weeks. Participants opting to continue to the OLT period will receive their first dose of open-label PF-06946860 at the Week 6 visit, coinciding with the last visit of the double-blind portion of the study.

epochs.name: Follow-up
epochs.label: Follow-up
epochs.description: Follow-up contact with all participants (whether participating in the 6-week double-blind only, or the OLT period) will be scheduled to occur at least 28 days and up to 35 days after the last administration of IP. For study Part A, this contact may be done via a phone call, telehealth, at home or in the clinic, per investigator judgement. For study part B, a home-HCP+TeleHealth (or in-Clinic) visit will be conducted. Additional follow-up may be conducted for safety evaluation, at the discretion of the investigator.

estimands.name: Estimand 1
estimands.description: Estimand 1 (similar to 'hypothetical') is intended to provide a population level estimate of the treatment effect on the change from baseline Appetite score for PF-06946860 compared to placebo in all evaluable participants under the scenario of no discontinuation of study intervention and without the potential confounding effects of prohibited medications, regardless of the participants' compliance with dosing (ie, using the Censored analysis set).
estimands.populationSummary: Population level estimate of the treatment effect on the change from baseline Appetite score for PF-06946860 compared to placebo in all evaluable participants.

studyDesigns.rationale: The primary purpose of this study is to assess the effect of repeated subcutaneous administrations of PF-06946860 on appetite in patients with advanced cancer, anorexia and elevated circulating GDF-15 levels. The study will also assess secondary and exploratory endpoints including fatigue, safety, body weight, immunogenicity, PK and PD.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: To evaluate the early effect of PF-06946860 compared to placebo on appetite in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01
endpoints.text: Change from baseline for patient reported 7-day recall Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score, at Week 4.
endpoints.description: The primary endpoint measures the change from baseline in the patient-reported 7-day recall Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score at Week 4.

objectives.description: The primary objective is to evaluate the early effect of PF-06946860 compared to placebo on appetite in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_03
objectives.text: To characterize the safety and tolerability of repeated subcutaneous administrations of PF-06946860 compared to placebo in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_03
endpoints.text: Incidence of adverse events and laboratory abnormalities, in Part A of the study.
endpoints.description: The secondary endpoint evaluates the incidence of adverse events and laboratory abnormalities in Part A of the study.

objectives.description: The secondary objective is to characterize the safety and tolerability of repeated subcutaneous administrations of PF-06946860 compared to placebo in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_02
objectives.text: To explore the relationship between pain and appetite and fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_02
endpoints.text: Change from baseline score for patient reported 7-day recall PROMIS-Pain 1a, Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Fatigue score and Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score at Weeks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint assesses the change from baseline score for patient reported 7-day recall PROMIS-Pain 1a, Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Fatigue score and Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score at Weeks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective is to explore the relationship between pain and appetite and fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_04
objectives.text: To characterize the unbound and total PK of PF-06946860 administered in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_04
endpoints.text: Serum unbound and total concentrations of PF-06946860, on Day 1 and Weeks 3, 4 and 6.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint measures serum unbound and total concentrations of PF-06946860 on Day 1 and Weeks 3, 4 and 6.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective is to characterize the unbound and total PK of PF-06946860 administered in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_05
objectives.text: To characterize the effect of PF-06946860 administration on circulating GDF-15 concentrations in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_05
endpoints.text: Serum concentrations of total GDF-15; and if feasible, unbound GDF-15, on Day 1 and Weeks 3, 4 and 6.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint measures serum concentrations of total GDF-15; and if feasible, unbound GDF-15, on Day 1 and Weeks 3, 4 and 6.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective is to characterize the effect of PF-06946860 administration on circulating GDF-15 concentrations in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_06
objectives.text: To assess the immunogenicity of PF-06946860 administered in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_06
endpoints.text: Incidence of ADA and, if applicable, NAb, in part A of the study.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint evaluates the incidence of ADA and, if applicable, NAb, in part A of the study.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective is to assess the immunogenicity of PF-06946860 administered in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_09
objectives.text: To characterize the safety and tolerability of repeated subcutaneous administrations of PF-06946860 in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer, during the open-label treatment period.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_09
endpoints.text: Incidence of adverse events and laboratory abnormalities, in Part B of the study.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint evaluates the incidence of adverse events and laboratory abnormalities in Part B of the study.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective for Part B is to characterize the safety and tolerability of repeated subcutaneous administrations of PF-06946860 in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer during the open-label treatment period.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_11
objectives.text: To explore the relationship between pain and appetite/fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer, during the open-label treatment period.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_11
endpoints.text: Change from baseline score for patient reported 7-day recall PROMIS-Pain 1a, Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Fatigue score and Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score, weekly from Week 7 to 24.
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint assesses the change from baseline score for patient reported 7-day recall PROMIS-Pain 1a, Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Fatigue score and Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite score, weekly from Week 7 to 24.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective for Part B is to explore the relationship between pain and appetite/fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer during the open-label treatment period.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_12
objectives.text: To assess patient global impression of change of appetite and fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer, during the open-label treatment period.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_12A
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint is PGI-C for appetite at Week 24.

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_12B
endpoints.description: The exploratory endpoint is PGI-C for fatigue at Week 24.

objectives.description: The tertiary/exploratory objective for Part B is to assess patient global impression of change of appetite and fatigue in participants with anorexia and advanced cancer during the open-label treatment period.

studyDesigns.description: This is a Phase 1b study in patients with advanced cancer, anorexia and elevated circulating GDF-15 levels with the primary purpose of assessing the effect of PF-06946860 administration on anorexia. The study will be conducted in 2 parts. The initial 6-week treatment period will be a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel group study.

footnotes.text: The investigator and participant must decide at the Week 4 visit if they wish to continue to the open-label treatment period.
Participants **not** continuing to Part B **only**. Follow-up contact 28 to 35 days from final dose of study drug. May occur via Clinic or TeleHealth+home-HCP visit, per PI judgement.
Participants continuing to Part B **only**. Follow-up contact 28 to 35 days from final dose of study drug. May occur via Clinic or TeleHealth+home-HCP visit, per PI judgement.

Participant and investigator may elect to have all post-randomization study visits for Part A conducted at-home or at clinic. A selection should be made, and efforts made, to have all Part A visits conducted using the same paradigm (home or clinic). If the participant chooses to do a visit at the clinic, the Telehealth visit will not be implemented. Additional Telehealth visits, home-HCP visits or phone calls may be scheduled, as needed per PI judgement. If operationally feasible, a nutritional consult via telehealth with a central vendor will be scheduled by the participant within the first 7 days after randomization.
This would only occur if a caregiver was available and willing to participate, and if the participant was in agreement with the caregiver completing the caregiver interview.

A full physical examination will be conducted at Screening. A symptom-directed PE may be conducted at any time during the study and/or at end of study as judged necessary by the investigator (eg, to assess previous findings or to assess ongoing/open AEs).
PRO (weekly) 7-day recall: appetite, fatigue, pain refers to the Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom assessments for appetite and fatigue and the PROMIS pain assessment.
Devices (eg, weighing scales and ePRO) required for study procedures will be used at Day 1 clinic visit, and provided to participant for at-home use, as applicable.

Every effort must be made to conduct procedures as specified, however if this becomes infeasible due to progression or complications of underlying disease, non-completion will not be considered a protocol deviation as long as the participant’s safety was preserved. Collection of safety samples should be prioritized.

Phone Interview will be conducted by central vendor within the 10 days following the Week 4 visit (ie, Day 29-39), or at early termination, if feasible. For Part B, a second participant interview will be conducted at week 12 ±10 days, or at early termination, if feasible.
On dosing days, blood samples for PK, GDF-15, immunogenicity and banked biospecimens will be collected prior to treatment administration.
If not collected on the designated collection day, collect at the next available time point when biospecimens are being collected in conjunction with a participant visit.

Participant and investigator may elect to have all post-randomization study visits for Part A conducted at-home or at clinic. A selection should be made, and efforts made, to have all Part A visits conducted using the same paradigm (home or clinic). If the participant chooses to do a visit at the clinic, the Telehealth visit will not be implemented. Additional Telehealth visits, home-HCP visits or phone calls may be scheduled, as needed per PI judgement. If operationally feasible, a nutritional consult via telehealth with a central vendor will be scheduled by the participant within the first 7 days after randomization.
This would only occur if a caregiver was available and willing to participate, and if the participant was in agreement with the caregiver completing the caregiver interview.

A full physical examination will be conducted at Screening. A symptom-directed PE may be conducted at any time during the study and/or at end of study as judged necessary by the investigator (eg, to assess previous findings or to assess ongoing/open AEs).
PRO (weekly) 7-day recall: appetite, fatigue, pain refers to the Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom assessments for appetite and fatigue and the PROMIS pain assessment.
Devices (eg, weighing scales and ePRO) required for study procedures will be used at Day 1 clinic visit, and provided to participant for at-home use, as applicable.

Every effort must be made to conduct procedures as specified, however if this becomes infeasible due to progression or complications of underlying disease, non-completion will not be considered a protocol deviation as long as the participant’s safety was preserved. Collection of safety samples should be prioritized.

Phone Interview will be conducted by central vendor within the 10 days following the Week 4 visit (ie, Day 29-39), or at early termination, if feasible. For Part B, a second participant interview will be conducted at week 12 ±10 days, or at early termination, if feasible.
On dosing days, blood samples for PK, GDF-15, immunogenicity and banked biospecimens will be collected prior to treatment administration.
If not collected on the designated collection day, collect at the next available time point when biospecimens are being collected in conjunction with a participant visit.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Male or female participants ≥ 18 (or the minimum country-specific age of consent if >18) at Screening. • Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Male or female participants ≥ 18 (or the minimum country-specific age of consent if >18) at Screening. • Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Woman who is pregnant or breast-feeding.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Woman who is pregnant or breast-feeding.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented diagnosis of non-small cell lung, pancreatic, colorectal, prostate, breast or ovarian cancer which, in the treating oncologist's assessment, is considered advanced, for example: • Unresectable Stage III or IV non-small cell lung cancer. • Locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma. • Metastatic or recurrent colorectal cancer. • Metastatic prostate cancer. • Metastatic or recurrent breast cancer. • Refractory ovarian cancer.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented diagnosis of non-small cell lung, pancreatic, colorectal, prostate, breast or ovarian cancer which, in the treating oncologist's assessment, is considered advanced, for example: • Unresectable Stage III or IV non-small cell lung cancer. • Locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma. • Metastatic or recurrent colorectal cancer. • Metastatic prostate cancer. • Metastatic or recurrent breast cancer. • Refractory ovarian cancer.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Meets any of the following criteria at Randomization: i. Not currently receiving antineoplastic therapy. This may be, for example, a patient who: • Has chosen not to receive further therapy, for example for quality of life reasons; • Is not indicated for future antineoplastic therapy or has exhausted all treatment options; • Has stable disease where no antineoplastic therapy is judged to be necessary; • Is taking a break between antineoplastic treatments, where the break has begun at least 4 weeks prior to Randomization and is anticipated to be longer than the 6-week duration of the double-blind portion of this study. If such a patient has recently completed a course of antineoplastic therapy, the last dose of that therapy should be at least 4 weeks prior to Randomization (see Section 6.5). - ii. Patients on standard of care systemic antineoplastic therapy or treatment without curative intent, (see Section 6.5).
eligibilityCriteria.text: Meets any of the following criteria at Randomization: i. Not currently receiving antineoplastic therapy. This may be, for example, a patient who: • Has chosen not to receive further therapy, for example for quality of life reasons; • Is not indicated for future antineoplastic therapy or has exhausted all treatment options; • Has stable disease where no antineoplastic therapy is judged to be necessary; • Is taking a break between antineoplastic treatments, where the break has begun at least 4 weeks prior to Randomization and is anticipated to be longer than the 6-week duration of the double-blind portion of this study. If such a patient has recently completed a course of antineoplastic therapy, the last dose of that therapy should be at least 4 weeks prior to Randomization (see Section 6.5). - ii. Patients on standard of care systemic antineoplastic therapy or treatment without curative intent, (see Section 6.5).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Anorexia as defined by a score in the Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite 7-day recall scale (see Appendix 7) at Screening and Randomization. CCI
eligibilityCriteria.text: Anorexia as defined by a score in the Cancer-Related Cachexia Symptom Assessment-Appetite 7-day recall scale (see Appendix 7) at Screening and Randomization. CCI

eligibilityCriteria.name: Serum GDF-15 levels of (as measured using the Investigational Use Only Roche Elecsys GDF 15 assay) 30
eligibilityCriteria.text: Serum GDF-15 levels of (as measured using the Investigational Use Only Roche Elecsys GDF 15 assay) 30

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants who are assessed by the investigator to be able to participate for the double-blind treatment period of 6 weeks . In the investigator's judgement, the participant should be anticipated to be able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures during the double-blind portion of the study. In cases where the investigator is not the treating oncologist, the oncologist's assessment of the potential participant's suitability for inclusion must also be considered.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants who are assessed by the investigator to be able to participate for the double-blind treatment period of 6 weeks . In the investigator's judgement, the participant should be anticipated to be able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures during the double-blind portion of the study. In cases where the investigator is not the treating oncologist, the oncologist's assessment of the potential participant's suitability for inclusion must also be considered.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix 1, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICD and in this protocol.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix 1, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICD and in this protocol.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Willing to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, contraception guidelines, and other study procedures for at least the 6-week double-blind treatment period.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Willing to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, contraception guidelines, and other study procedures for at least the 6-week double-blind treatment period.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Receiving tube feedings or parenteral nutrition (either total or partial) at the time of Screening or Randomization.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Receiving tube feedings or parenteral nutrition (either total or partial) at the time of Screening or Randomization.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Current active reversible causes of decreased food intake, as determined by the Investigator. These causes may include, but are not limited to: - Mechanical obstructions making patient unable to eat; - National Cancer Institute (NCI) Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 3 or 4 oral mucositis.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Current active reversible causes of decreased food intake, as determined by the Investigator. These causes may include, but are not limited to: - Mechanical obstructions making patient unable to eat; - National Cancer Institute (NCI) Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 3 or 4 oral mucositis.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Unresolved acute effects of any prior therapy, or uncontrolled symptoms (eg, pain, diarrhea) at Randomization that in the judgement of the PI makes the participant unsuitable for the study. For example, uncontrolled pain may be indicated by a recent (eg, within past 15 days) change in opioid treatment.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Unresolved acute effects of any prior therapy, or uncontrolled symptoms (eg, pain, diarrhea) at Randomization that in the judgement of the PI makes the participant unsuitable for the study. For example, uncontrolled pain may be indicated by a recent (eg, within past 15 days) change in opioid treatment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Current, severe gastrointestinal disease (including esophagitis, gastritis, malabsorption) at Randomization.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Current, severe gastrointestinal disease (including esophagitis, gastritis, malabsorption) at Randomization.

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of gastrectomy.
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of gastrectomy.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Persistent symptoms due to ascites at randomization, unresponsive to clinically indicated interventions.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Persistent symptoms due to ascites at randomization, unresponsive to clinically indicated interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with known symptomatic brain metastases requiring steroids. Participants with asymptomatic or previously diagnosed brain metastases are eligible if they have completed their treatment and have recovered from the acute effects of radiation therapy or surgery prior to Screening, have discontinued corticosteroid treatment for these metastases and are neurologically stable for at least 4 weeks (requires magnetic resonance imaging [MRI] confirmation) prior to Screening. Note that if radiation therapy becomes necessary for treatment of brain metastases or palliative treatment after Randomization, this will not be a reason for discontinuation.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with known symptomatic brain metastases requiring steroids. Participants with asymptomatic or previously diagnosed brain metastases are eligible if they have completed their treatment and have recovered from the acute effects of radiation therapy or surgery prior to Screening, have discontinued corticosteroid treatment for these metastases and are neurologically stable for at least 4 weeks (requires magnetic resonance imaging [MRI] confirmation) prior to Screening. Note that if radiation therapy becomes necessary for treatment of brain metastases or palliative treatment after Randomization, this will not be a reason for discontinuation.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection, including but not limited to HBV, HCV, HIV or participants with known AIDS-related illness.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection, including but not limited to HBV, HCV, HIV or participants with known AIDS-related illness.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Undergoing major surgery (central venous access placement and tumor biopsies are not considered major surgery) within 4 weeks prior to first dose of investigational product. Patient must be well recovered from acute effects of surgery prior to screening. Patient should not have plans to undergo major surgical procedures during the study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Undergoing major surgery (central venous access placement and tumor biopsies are not considered major surgery) within 4 weeks prior to first dose of investigational product. Patient must be well recovered from acute effects of surgery prior to screening. Patient should not have plans to undergo major surgical procedures during the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Other medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Initiation of new systemic antineoplastic therapy (eg, chemotherapy, immunotherapy) within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or planning to initiate antineoplastic therapy during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Initiation of new systemic antineoplastic therapy (eg, chemotherapy, immunotherapy) within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or planning to initiate antineoplastic therapy during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Initiation of new treatment prescribed or self-administered and known to have an impact on anorexia, weight loss or cachexia, including but not limited to mirtazapine, olanzapine, cannabinoids, within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study; stable (ie, no significant change to dosage or frequency of administration in prior 4 weeks) therapy is permissible. See Section 6.5 Concomitant Therapy for details.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Initiation of new treatment prescribed or self-administered and known to have an impact on anorexia, weight loss or cachexia, including but not limited to mirtazapine, olanzapine, cannabinoids, within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study; stable (ie, no significant change to dosage or frequency of administration in prior 4 weeks) therapy is permissible. See Section 6.5 Concomitant Therapy for details.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Radiation therapy other than localized palliative treatment (eg, pain management for bone metastases) within the 3 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Radiation therapy other than localized palliative treatment (eg, pain management for bone metastases) within the 3 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Initiation of new treatment with systemic glucocorticoids within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study; stable (ie, no significant change to dosage or frequency of administration in prior 4 weeks) steroid therapy eg, dexamethasone as part of pre-medication or daily oral prednisone is permissible. See Section 6.5 Concomitant Therapy for details.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Initiation of new treatment with systemic glucocorticoids within the 4 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study; stable (ie, no significant change to dosage or frequency of administration in prior 4 weeks) steroid therapy eg, dexamethasone as part of pre-medication or daily oral prednisone is permissible. See Section 6.5 Concomitant Therapy for details.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Initiation of megestrol acetate within the 2 weeks prior to the first dose of investigational product or during the 6-week double-blind portion of the study; stable (ie, no significant change to dosage or frequency of administration in prior 2 weeks) therapy is permissible. See Section 6.5 Concomitant Therapy for details.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Received previous treatment with at least one dose of PF-06946860.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Received previous treatment with at least one dose of PF-06946860.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Actively receiving a concurrent investigational agent, or previous administration with an investigational drug within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of investigational product used in this study (whichever is longer).
eligibilityCriteria.text: Actively receiving a concurrent investigational agent, or previous administration with an investigational drug within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of investigational product used in this study (whichever is longer).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Positive urine drug screen for illicit drugs at Screening, with the exception of cannabinoids per Prior/Concomitant Therapy criteria number 12 above. Results may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary. In addition, drugs which have been medically prescribed and reported to the investigator at the Screening visit may be permitted, if judged appropriate by the investigator and permitted by the Sponsor. Prescribed pain medications (eg, opioids, morphine, hydrocodone) do not need be cleared with the Sponsor.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Positive urine drug screen for illicit drugs at Screening, with the exception of cannabinoids per Prior/Concomitant Therapy criteria number 12 above. Results may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary. In addition, drugs which have been medically prescribed and reported to the investigator at the Screening visit may be permitted, if judged appropriate by the investigator and permitted by the Sponsor. Prescribed pain medications (eg, opioids, morphine, hydrocodone) do not need be cleared with the Sponsor.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Inadequate liver function at Screening as evidenced by any 1 of the following, which may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary:  Total serum bilirubin >1.5 x upper limit of normal (ULN) unless the participant has documented Gilbert syndrome;  Aspartate (AST) and/or Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) >3 x ULN; >5.0 x ULN if there is liver involvement by the tumor;  Alkaline phosphatase >3 x ULN. Inclusion of participants with alkaline phosphatase >3 x ULN may be acceptable if they are deemed medically stable and fit for the study by both investigator and sponsor medically qualified personnel.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Inadequate liver function at Screening as evidenced by any 1 of the following, which may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary:  Total serum bilirubin >1.5 x upper limit of normal (ULN) unless the participant has documented Gilbert syndrome;  Aspartate (AST) and/or Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) >3 x ULN; >5.0 x ULN if there is liver involvement by the tumor;  Alkaline phosphatase >3 x ULN. Inclusion of participants with alkaline phosphatase >3 x ULN may be acceptable if they are deemed medically stable and fit for the study by both investigator and sponsor medically qualified personnel.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Inadequate renal function at Screening, including creatinine >2 mg/dL, or calculated Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) <30 mL/minute/1.73 m 2 as calculated by the modification of diet in renal disease (MDRD) equation. 31 Result may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Inadequate renal function at Screening, including creatinine >2 mg/dL, or calculated Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) <30 mL/minute/1.73 m 2 as calculated by the modification of diet in renal disease (MDRD) equation. 31 Result may be confirmed by a single repeat, if necessary.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.

biospecimenRetentions.name: Biospecimen Banking
biospecimenRetentions.label: Banked Biospecimens
biospecimenRetentions.description: Banked biospecimens will be collected for exploratory pharmacogenomic/genomic/biomarker analyses and retained in the Biospecimen Banking System (BBS).

studyInterventions.name: PF-06946860

role.decode: Investigational Product - the novel drug or treatment being evaluated in the study

type.decode: Biologic, antibody, protein, cell therapy, or gene therapy product

studyInterventions.description: Investigational biologic administered subcutaneously every 3 weeks in the double-blind and optional open-label phases to assess effects on appetite and fatigue in patients with cancer-related anorexia.

administrations.name: Every 3 weeks subcutaneous regimen

duration.text: Up to 24 weeks total (6 weeks double-blind with 2 doses, optional open-label extension with up to 7 additional doses)
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participation in the open-label extension (Part B) is optional.

administrations.description: Each dose is comprised of two 1 mL SC injections which are to be administered consecutively by appropriately qualified health care personnel.

studyInterventions.name: Placebo

role.decode: Inactive control substance matching the investigational product

studyInterventions.description: Matching placebo administered subcutaneously every 3 weeks in the double-blind phase.

administrations.name: Every 3 weeks subcutaneous regimen
administrations.description: Each dose is comprised of two 1 mL SC injections which are to be administered consecutively by appropriately qualified health care personnel.
